thesis: Undergoing dramatic transformation from distressed wireless carrier to spectrum monetization entity. $42.6B in strategic asset sales to AT&T ($22.65B) and SpaceX (~$20B) resolves 2026 debt maturity wall. Retains ~2% SpaceX equity stake worth ~$11B. EchoStar Capital division formed to manage space economy M&A. Pay-TV (DISH TV + Sling: 7M subscribers) generates $2.69B Adjusted OIBDA but declining. Boost Mobile transitioning to 'hybrid MNO' on AT&T network.

drivers: SpaceX IPO at $200B+ valuation (2x current) — stake worth $20B+
Successful Boost Mobile turnaround on AT&T network
Deleveraging complete — debt-free spectrum holding company
Multiple expansion to 3-4x EBITDA on clean balance sheet

risks: FCC or DOJ blocks major transaction (low probability, high impact)
Pay-TV decline accelerates faster than expected
Forced asset sales at unfavorable prices

base_case.assumptions: Deals close as planned by mid-2026; Debt reduced to $5-8B; Pay-TV decline moderates; Boost stabilizes on AT&T network; SpaceX stake maintains current $11B value; Modest dividend potential by 2027-2028.

catalysts.event: AT&T Deal FCC Approval — Critical regulatory milestone
SpaceX Deal Close — $11B+ equity stake formalized

key_risks: Regulatory approval risk for $42.6B spectrum deals
Infrastructure creditor protests from American Tower, Crown Castle
Pay-TV acceleration — DISH TV lost 168K subs in Q4 2025
Starlink competition crushing HughesNet (97% of speed tests)
Complex 'sum-of-parts' valuation dependent on SpaceX IPO timing
